items.raw_claim: US government issued export control directive suspending Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access for foreign nationals
items.summary: The US government issued an export control directive suspending access to Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the US. This is unprecedented — a government mandating access restrictions on specific AI models. If you rely on these models or serve international users, you need fallback plans immediately. The directive's scope and duration remain unclear.
items.title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.uncertainty: Duration and exact legal scope of the directive are not yet clear
items.verdict_reason: Major policy change directly affecting Claude model access
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly impacts anyone using Claude's frontier models or serving international users

items.raw_claim: AI SDK 7 introduces HarnessAgent for swapping between Claude Code, Codex, and Pi harnesses via unified API
items.summary: Vercel's AI SDK 7 introduces HarnessAgent, a unified API to swap between agent harnesses (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) without rewriting code. Write the agent once and switch harnesses via config. Relevant if you're building agent orchestration — reduces lock-in to any single harness.
items.title: Program Claude Code, Codex, Pi and other agent harnesses with AI SDK
items.uncertainty: Experimental release; adapter coverage may be limited initially
items.verdict_reason: New abstraction layer for agent harnesses with practical lock-in reduction
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Practical tool for reducing agent harness lock-in

items.raw_claim: 21 zero-day vulnerabilities disclosed in FFmpeg across multiple codecs and formats
items.summary: Security researchers disclosed 21 zero-day vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the ubiquitous media processing library. If you use FFmpeg in any pipeline (video transcoding, media processing, thumbnail generation), check your version and update. The vulnerabilities span multiple codecs and formats.
items.title: 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg
items.uncertainty: Patch status and affected version ranges need verification
items.verdict_reason: Significant security disclosure in widely-used library
items.worth_mentioning_reason: FFmpeg is ubiquitous; 21 zero-days is a significant security event

items.raw_claim: Postgres 19 preview highlights time-related improvements and new features coming later this year
items.summary: Preview of Postgres 19 features, including improvements to time-related functionality. Postgres 19 is expected to ship later this year. Worth tracking if you're on Postgres — new features may influence schema design or query patterns.
items.title: Looking Forward to Postgres 19: It's About Time
items.uncertainty: Feature list may change before final release
items.verdict_reason: Practical infrastructure preview for Postgres users
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Postgres is core infrastructure for many solo devs

items.raw_claim: WASI 0.3 launched with async support and component model integration for WebAssembly
items.summary: WASI 0.3 has launched, bringing async support and component model integration to WebAssembly's system interface. This is a significant milestone for portable server-side Wasm — makes it practical to build composable services that run anywhere.
items.title: WASI 0.3 Launched
items.uncertainty: Ecosystem tooling and runtime support may still be catching up
items.verdict_reason: Significant infrastructure milestone for WebAssembly ecosystem
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Major Wasm milestone that makes server-side Wasm more practical

items.raw_claim: Kimi K2.7 Code from Moonshot AI available on Vercel AI Gateway for long-horizon coding tasks
items.summary: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.7 Code is now available on Vercel AI Gateway. It's a coding model designed for long-horizon programming tasks with native multimodal support (text + vision). Always runs in thinking mode. Another option if you're evaluating coding model alternatives.
items.title: Kimi K2.7 Code now available on AI Gateway
items.verdict_reason: Narrower scope — one more coding model option
items.worth_mentioning_reason: New coding model option with multimodal support

items.raw_claim: OpenAI WebRTC audio session tool now supports feeding document context into realtime voice conversations
items.summary: Simon Willison updated his OpenAI WebRTC audio tool to support document context — you can now feed documents into a realtime audio session. Useful reference if you're building voice interfaces that need to reason over documents.
items.title: OpenAI WebRTC Audio Session, now with document context
items.verdict_reason: Useful reference for voice agent builders but narrower audience
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Relevant to voice agent development patterns

private_memo:
# Morning memo — 2026-06-14

**Source failures:** None observed.

## Worth attention

- **Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/13/us-government-directive-to-suspend-access/#atom-everything
  The US government issued an export control directive suspending access to Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the US. This is unprecedented — a government mandating access restrictions on specific AI models. If you rely on these models or serve international users, you need fallback plans immediately.

- **Program Claude Code, Codex, Pi and other agent harnesses with AI SDK** — https://vercel.com/changelog/program-agent-harnesses-with-ai-sdk
  AI SDK 7 introduces HarnessAgent, a single API for running established agent harnesses including Claude Code, Codex, and Pi. Write the agent once, swap harnesses via config. Reduces lock-in if you're building agent orchestration.

- **21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg** — https://depthfirst.com/research/21-zero-days-in-ffmpeg
  Security researchers disclosed 21 zero-day vulnerabilities in FFmpeg across multiple codecs and formats. If you use FFmpeg anywhere in your stack, check your version and patch.

- **Looking Forward to Postgres 19: It's About Time** — https://www.pgedge.com/blog/looking-forward-to-postgres-19-its-about-time
  Preview of Postgres 19 features with focus on time-related improvements. Worth tracking for schema design decisions.

- **WASI 0.3 Launched** — https://bytecodealliance.org/articles/WASI-0.3
  WASI 0.3 ships async support and component model integration for WebAssembly. Makes portable server-side Wasm more practical.

- **Kimi K2.7 Code now available on AI Gateway** — https://vercel.com/changelog/kimi-k2-7-code-now-available-on-ai-gateway
  Moonshot AI's K2.7 Code is a multimodal coding model for long-horizon tasks, now on Vercel AI Gateway. Another option if evaluating coding models.

- **OpenAI WebRTC Audio Session, now with document context** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/12/openai-webrtc/#atom-everything
  Simon Willison's WebRTC audio tool now supports document context in realtime voice sessions. Useful reference for voice interfaces that reason over documents.

- **How to setup a local coding agent on macOS** — https://ikyle.me/blog/2026/how-to-setup-a-local-coding-agent-on-macos
  Guide for setting up local coding agents on macOS with local models. Relevant if running Ollama-based coding setups.

- **Slightly reducing the sloppiness of AI generated front end** — https://envs.net/~volpe/blog/posts/reduce-slop.html
  Practical tips for reducing common quality issues in AI-generated frontend code.

## Full digest
- [P] [simon-willison] Statement on US govt directive to suspend Fable 5/Mythos 5 — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/13/us-government-directive-to-suspend-access/#atom-everything — Export control directive suspends models for foreign nationals
- [P] [vercel-changelog] Program Claude Code, Codex, Pi with AI SDK — https://vercel.com/changelog/program-agent-harnesses-with-ai-sdk — AI SDK 7 HarnessAgent unifies harness switching
- [P] [lobste.rs] 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg — https://depthfirst.com/research/21-zero-days-in-ffmpeg — 21 zero-day vulnerabilities across codecs
- [P] [hn] Looking Forward to Postgres 19 — https://www.pgedge.com/blog/looking-forward-to-postgres-19-its-about-time — Postgres 19 time improvements preview
- [P] [hn] WASI 0.3 Launched — https://bytecodealliance.org/articles/WASI-0.3 — Async and component model for Wasm
- [P] [vercel-changelog] Kimi K2.7 Code on AI Gateway — https://vercel.com/changelog/kimi-k2-7-code-now-available-on-ai-gateway — Multimodal coding model for long-horizon tasks
- [P] [simon-willison] OpenAI WebRTC Audio with document context — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/12/openai-webrtc/#atom-everything — Voice sessions now support document context
- [P] [hn] Local coding agent on macOS — https://ikyle.me/blog/2026/how-to-setup-a-local-coding-agent-on-macos — Guide for local coding agent setup
- [P] [hn] Reducing AI frontend sloppiness — https://envs.net/~volpe/blog/posts/reduce-slop.html — Tips for cleaner AI-generated UI
- [M] [hn] H.R. 6028 Copyright Office overhaul — https://www.eff.org/deeplinks/2026/06/congress-just-rushed-through-disastrous-copyright-office-overhaul — Copyright Office restructuring
- [M] [hn] Malware with nuclear/bio text in spyware — https://twitter.com/jsrailton/status/2064661778978533571 — Supply chain risk: weaponized npm packages
- [M] [hn] erm: CLI to strip filler words — https://doug.sh/posts/erm-a-local-cli-that-strips-ums-uhs-and-erms-from-speech/ — Local tool for cleaning speech recordings
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Fable 5 suspended on AI Gateway — https://vercel.com/changelog/claude-fable-5-access-suspended-on-ai-gateway — Duplicate: covered above
- [R] [gh-nextjs] v16.3.0-canary.50 — https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v16.3.0-canary.50 — Canary patch bump
- [R] [simon-willison] Quoting Andrew Singleton — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/12/andrew-singleton/#atom-everything — Commentary, not actionable
- [R] [hn] Townsquare release — https://cauenapier.com/blog/townsquare_release/ — Niche social tool
- [R] [hn] Rejected Emoji Proposals — https://charlottebuff.com/unicode/misc/rejected-emoji-proposals/ — Entertainment
- [R] [lobste.rs] Swift TrueType Hinting — https://swift.org/blog/migrating-truetype-hinting-to-swift/ — Niche Apple internals

- [R] [lobste.rs] Lispy DSLs (2012) — https://www.more-magic.net/posts/lispy-dsl-scss.html — Old content
- [R] [hn] Shadow over Fable — https://12gramsofcarbon.com/p/tech-things-there-is-a-massive-shadow — Duplicate Fable coverage
- [R] [hn] Fable statement (HN) — https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access — Duplicate
- [R] [hn] Electric motors without rare earths — https://www.renaultgroup.com/en/magazine/energy-and-powertrains/all-about-electric-motors-with-no-rare-earths/ — Off-topic
